reviewer: weekly-hardening-routine (headless Chromium 141 via CDP; no chrome-devtools-mcp in cloud env)

spec_match.rationale: Implements the explainer's button-invoker pattern with command=toggle-menu/commandfor and reads back the real popover/ARIA state.

interactive.rationale: Verified both paths: flagged (real :popover-open toggling, toggle event) and unflagged (labelled JS fallback with explicit aria-expanded).

edge_cases.rationale: Implicit-vs-explicit ARIA distinction is explained and shown, but focus-moves-into-menu behaviour promised by the explainer was not asserted in the flagged run.

conformance.rationale: invoker-commands-base + interface assertions in the feature suite.

runtime_evidence.rationale: Dual-config CDP runs; open-state screenshot inspected (menu adjacent to invoker, no detached overlay).

telemetry_health.rationale: State-read assert wired; KV not queried.

accessibility.rationale: Fallback path sets explicit aria-haspopup/aria-expanded; real path relies on implicit semantics which were reported but not captured from the accessibility tree.

summary: Invoker pattern demonstrated honestly in both configurations.

openQuestions.title: Assert focus lands in the open menulist
openQuestions.detail: The explainer says keyboard focus should move to the first focusable element of the rendered menulist; in a flagged session verify document.activeElement after toggle-menu and record it in the readout.
